title: International Resilience Congress
content: idRc 2026 hosted by Middle East Technical University with the theme of “Resilience in the age of Information Technologies” will be held at METU Culture and Convention Center on 2 - 4 November.
snippet: idRc 2026 hosted by Middle East Technical University with the theme of “Resilience in the age of Information Technologies” will be held at METU Culture and Convention Center on 2 - 4 November.

title: Predicting hydrological drought at global scale: an analysis of the CEMS seasonal forecasts
content: This study evaluates the performance of forecasts in detecting hydrological drought in the period 1991–2022 using the ECMWF reanalysis ERA5 as a proxy for the observational reference.
snippet: This study evaluates the performance of forecasts in detecting hydrological drought in the period 1991–2022 using the ECMWF reanalysis ERA5 as a proxy for the observational reference.

title: Rethinking architectural education post-disaster: an analytical framework for curricular transformation after the 2023 Kahramanmaraş earthquakes
content: This study conducts a comparative analysis of the presence and evolution of disaster and earthquake-focused courses in Turkish public and foundation universities between 2022 and 2025.
snippet: This study conducts a comparative analysis of the presence and evolution of disaster and earthquake-focused courses in Turkish public and foundation universities between 2022 and 2025.

title: A novel approach to quantify cover-collapse sinkhole occurrences using multitemporal lidar
content: The researchers developed a novel method that detects elevation changes between two successive high-resolution lidar-derived digital elevation models and analyzes these changes to identify cover-collapse sinkholes that occurred over a defined time frame.
snippet: The researchers developed a novel method that detects elevation changes between two successive high-resolution lidar-derived digital elevation models and analyzes these changes to identify

title: STC-Specialist-Fund Operations, Risk Management & Oversight
content: CDRI established a multi-donor trust fund, the Infrastructure Resilience Accelerator Fund (IRAF), in November 2022, hosted by the United Nations Multi-Partner Trust Fund Office (UN MPTFO) for a period of five years.
snippet: CDRI established a multi-donor trust fund, the Infrastructure Resilience Accelerator Fund (IRAF), in November 2022, hosted by the United Nations Multi-Partner Trust Fund Office (UN MPTFO) for a period

title: Spatially-explicit economic impacts and risks of climate change in Europe
content: This paper improves climate-impact assessment for Europe by integrating NUTS-2 level damage functions from the ICES computable general equilibrium model into the spatially explicit CLIMRISK integrated assessment model.
snippet: This paper improves climate-impact assessment for Europe by integrating NUTS-2 level damage functions from the ICES computable general equilibrium model into the spatially explicit CLIMRISK integrated

title: Evolution of earthquake crisis management and recovery in Italy after 2009: An expert commentary
content: This paper presents an expert commentary on lessons learned across five dimensions: governance, community engagement, technical assessment tools, professional mobilization, and urban reconstruction.
snippet: This paper presents an expert commentary on lessons learned across five dimensions: governance, community engagement, technical assessment tools, professional mobilization, and urban reconstruction.

title: Spatio-temporal variability of hydrometeorological disasters in Indonesia based on national disaster risk management data
content: This study examines the relationship between hydrometeorological disasters and climate variability modes using disaster event ratios from the Indonesian National Disaster Management Agency (BNPB) for 2008–2023 in Indonesia.
snippet: This study examines the relationship between hydrometeorological disasters and climate variability modes using disaster event ratios from the Indonesian National Disaster Management Agency (BNPB) for

title: Heat–health action plans: guidance, second edition
content: This guidance on heat–health action plans (HHAPs) supports countries in developing, strengthening and implementing such plans at national, regional and local levels.
snippet: This guidance on heat–health action plans (HHAPs) supports countries in developing, strengthening and implementing such plans at national, regional and local levels.

title: What measles reveals about health security in Southeast Asia
content: A measles resurgence shows immunity gaps and is a barometer for health system resilience following the Covid-19 pandemic.
snippet: A measles resurgence shows immunity gaps and is a barometer for health system resilience following the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: Flint Water Crisis: Leadership Lessons for Drinking Water Disaster Risk Reduction
content: This online training examines the Flint Water Crisis as a case study in technological and infrastructure-related disaster risk affecting public water systems and public health.
snippet: This online training examines the Flint Water Crisis as a case study in technological and infrastructure-related disaster risk affecting public water systems and public health.

title: Dong Thap implements soft, "nature-friendly" solutions to respond to landslides
content: Dong Thap province is facing numerous challenges due to the impacts of climate change, subsidence, landslides, flooding, drought, and saltwater intrusion.
snippet: Dong Thap province is facing numerous challenges due to the impacts of climate change, subsidence, landslides, flooding, drought, and saltwater intrusion.

title: Insurance cover growing in line with increasing catastrophe risk: Swiss Re
content: While the global natural catastrophe insurance protection gap is estimated to have risen to US $424 billion in 2025, new research from Swiss Re suggests that insurance coverage is growing broadly in line with the increasing catastrophe exposure.
snippet: While the global natural catastrophe insurance protection gap is estimated to have risen to US $424 billion in 2025, new research from Swiss Re suggests that insurance coverage is growing broadly in

title: Principles and processes for prioritising and influencing urban resilience investment
content: This guide outlines a set of principles and processes for resilience investment in Australia aimed at fostering effective, sustainable, and long-term resilience-building initiatives.
snippet: This guide outlines a set of principles and processes for resilience investment in Australia aimed at fostering effective, sustainable, and long-term resilience-building initiatives.

title: Prescribed fires can cut smoke pollution for years, miles beyond burn areas
content: A Stanford-led study based on two decades of satellite data finds California could cut deadly pollution from wildfire smoke by 20% in active fire years by expanding use of prescribed fire in conifer forests each year.
snippet: A Stanford-led study based on two decades of satellite data finds California could cut deadly pollution from wildfire smoke by 20% in active fire years by expanding use of prescribed fire in conifer

title: What do we know about Europe's early and intense heatwave in May 2026?
content: Western Europe experienced an unusually early and intense heatwave during the second half of May 2026, mainly between 21 and 30 May.
snippet: Western Europe experienced an unusually early and intense heatwave during the second half of May 2026, mainly between 21 and 30 May.

title: AIEP Editore
content: Founded in 1980, the publishing house dedicated itself for at least ten years to publishing texts on the history, culture, art, and law of the Republic of San Marino.
snippet: Founded in 1980, the publishing house dedicated itself for at least ten years to publishing texts on the history, culture, art, and law of the Republic of San Marino.

title: Interoperability in operation
content: Learn about the how to co-design interoperable tools to support operational decision-making for climate extremes.
snippet: Learn about the how to co-design interoperable tools to support operational decision-making for climate extremes.

title: Public risk communication
content: Learn how to implement a wildfire awareness campaign, use arts and creative engagement and immersive tools for public risk communication.
snippet: Learn how to implement a wildfire awareness campaign, use arts and creative engagement and immersive tools for public risk communication.

title: As wildfires increase in the West, so does suppression spending
content: A new study projects the intertwined relationship between fires and the money spent fighting them.
snippet: A new study projects the intertwined relationship between fires and the money spent fighting them.

title: 20th Integrated Water Resources and Flood Risk Management for Climate Resilience Face-to-Face training
content: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to Integrated Flood Risk Management (IFRM) under a rapidly changing climate.
snippet: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to Integrated Flood Risk Management (IFRM) under a rapidly changing climate.

title: Building Infrastructure Resilience in IORA Member States - Webinar 3: Financing Resilient Infrastructure
content: Financing resilience requires a new generation of innovation—one that moves beyond short-term recovery models and toward proactive, long-term adaptation strategies.
snippet: Financing resilience requires a new generation of innovation—one that moves beyond short-term recovery models and toward proactive, long-term adaptation strategies.

title: Japan government approves plan to more than halve Tokyo area quake deaths
content: Japan's government has approved a revised plan to prepare for a major earthquake centered on the greater Tokyo area.
snippet: Japan's government has approved a revised plan to prepare for a major earthquake centered on the greater Tokyo area.

title: Climate change reshapes Spain's rockfall risk as frost weathering moves uphill
content: Climate change is altering where and when rocks are most likely to fracture across Spain, according to new research that suggests warming temperatures are redistributing a key process responsible for breaking down mountain landscapes.

title: Could a controversial cooling strategy save our fisheries and coral reefs from marine heat waves?
content: As the planet continues to warm, marine heat waves are growing longer and deadlier, hurting the seafood supply that billions of people worldwide rely on for their food and livelihoods.
snippet: As the planet continues to warm, marine heat waves are growing longer and deadlier, hurting the seafood supply that billions of people worldwide rely on for their food and livelihoods.

title: ‘It’s going to be extremely hot’: workers imperiled as sweltering World Cup temperatures are forecast
content: It could top 90F in several cities hosting World Cup games – and workers could pay the price with their health.
snippet: It could top 90F in several cities hosting World Cup games – and workers could pay the price with their health.

title: Understanding heatwaves: beyond extreme temperatures
content: Understanding and monitoring extreme thermal conditions – shaped by a combination of temperature, humidity, sunshine, and wind speed – is essential to help protect human life.
snippet: Understanding and monitoring extreme thermal conditions – shaped by a combination of temperature, humidity, sunshine, and wind speed – is essential to help protect human life.

title: The Netherlands vs the sea: The race to hold back rising waters
content: The Netherlands has spent decades building and perfecting one of the most sophisticated water-defense systems in the world.
snippet: The Netherlands has spent decades building and perfecting one of the most sophisticated water-defense systems in the world.

title: Advancing gender-transformative action in human mobility in the context of climate change
content: This publication explores how climate mobility programmes can move beyond gender-responsive approaches towards more gender-transformative change.
snippet: This publication explores how climate mobility programmes can move beyond gender-responsive approaches towards more gender-transformative change.

title: Researchers find trees can shield homes from storms
content: The study showed that trees around a low-rise building can reduce the wind force on segments by as much as 50%.
snippet: The study showed that trees around a low-rise building can reduce the wind force on segments by as much as 50%.

title: Extreme heat: a new red card for sports?
content: Extreme heat is reshaping sports and sport governance faster than many events are ready for. In this article, experts Ollie Jay, Lachlan McIver, Alejandro Saez Reale and Marc Gordon set out what’s at stake.

title: Lowy Institute
content: The Lowy Institute is a leading global think tank that looks at the world from Australia’s perspective.
snippet: The Lowy Institute is a leading global think tank that looks at the world from Australia’s perspective.

title: Fishers live in fear as looming El Niño and fuel crisis pile on pressure
content: From the Philippines to Peru a vital industry already reeling from multiple problems faces devastation from skyrocketing fuel prices and super-charged Pacific warming.
snippet: From the Philippines to Peru a vital industry already reeling from multiple problems faces devastation from skyrocketing fuel prices and super-charged Pacific warming.

title: "The heat is gone, but the drought remains."
content: Too little rain and snow, too little water in the rivers: An interview with SLF hydrologist Manuela Brunner on the causes of the ongoing drought in Switzerland.
snippet: Too little rain and snow, too little water in the rivers: An interview with SLF hydrologist Manuela Brunner on the causes of the ongoing drought in Switzerland.

title: Earthquakes can be destructive for distant cities built on top of basins – now we know why
content: Sedimentary basins – depressions in Earth’s crust caused by tectonic activity – tend to be flat and are favoured places to build cities. But during earthquakes, they can become natural resonance chambers.
snippet: Sedimentary basins – depressions in Earth’s crust caused by tectonic activity – tend to be flat and are favoured places to build cities. But during earthquakes, they can become natural resonance

title: Going up against the heat: Vertical greenery keeps cities cool
content: Green wall installation can provide cooling effects for both indoor and outdoor environments.
snippet: Green wall installation can provide cooling effects for both indoor and outdoor environments.

title: Amidst the heat and breeze: a documentation of traditional cooling practices in Sudanese houses
content: This illustrated booklet documents the enduring wisdom of traditional Sudanese homes and the indigenous, low-cost techniques communities have long used to stay cool during extreme heat.
snippet: This illustrated booklet documents the enduring wisdom of traditional Sudanese homes and the indigenous, low-cost techniques communities have long used to stay cool during extreme heat.

title: 6 ways this year’s “Super El Niño” could affect climate, humans, and marine creatures
content: NOAA defines a “very strong” El Niño as when the Pacific’s surface waters are more than 2°C warmer than average. What does this mean for climate, for humans, and marine species?
snippet: NOAA defines a “very strong” El Niño as when the Pacific’s surface waters are more than 2°C warmer than average. What does this mean for climate, for humans, and marine species?

title: A century of chemical weapons: use, prohibition, and elimination
content: This monograph provides a comprehensive examination of the history, regulation, and elimination of chemical weapons over the past century.
snippet: This monograph provides a comprehensive examination of the history, regulation, and elimination of chemical weapons over the past century.

title: The 2026 World Cup could be the hottest yet
content: A new analysis finds climate change is increasing the odds of dangerous heat during many 2026 World Cup matches, putting players, fans, and workers at greater risk.
snippet: A new analysis finds climate change is increasing the odds of dangerous heat during many 2026 World Cup matches, putting players, fans, and workers at greater risk.

title: Millions of homes in London, Essex and Kent at risk of sinking as climate crisis worsens
content: Analysis pinpoints areas most vulnerable to hotter, drier weather causing ground to shrink and drag foundations down
snippet: Analysis pinpoints areas most vulnerable to hotter, drier weather causing ground to shrink and drag foundations down

title: White paper on live wildlife trade and markets
content: The paper examines the complex links between live wildlife trade, zoonotic disease risks, biodiversity loss, animal welfare, and global health security through a comprehensive One Health perspective.
snippet: The paper examines the complex links between live wildlife trade, zoonotic disease risks, biodiversity loss, animal welfare, and global health security through a comprehensive One Health perspective.

title: International Alliance against Health Risks in Wildlife Trade
content: The International Alliance against Health Risks in Wildlife Trade  is an open, inclusive and collaborative space where stakeholders join forces to better understand and reduce the threat of pathogen spillover from wildlife trade and markets.
snippet: The International Alliance against Health Risks in Wildlife Trade is an open, inclusive and collaborative space where stakeholders join forces to better understand and reduce the threat of pathogen

title: Knowing what the weather will do: A guide for practitioners on impact-based early warning
content: This guidebook provides a novel approach and methodology for designing and implementing Impact-based early warning (IbEW) systems. it guides the co-development and integration of risk knowledge on hazards, exposure, vulnerabilities

title: Uncertainty, pastoral knowledge and early warning: a review of drought management in the drylands, with insights from northern Kenya
content: This study assesses global inequalities in exposure to compound precipitation extremes (CPEs), defined as the concurrent occurrence of short-duration, high-intensity 1-day precipitation and prolonged 5-day precipitation.
